controls.name: Account-level Public Access Block

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.reversibility: Easy (CLI command). Terraform state is consistent.
safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: High if existing workloads rely on public buckets
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Account-wide S3 Public Access Block affects all buckets in the account. While reversible, it can abruptly break public workloads across the entire account.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Account-level scope; preflight does not dynamically find ALL public dependencies.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Missing IAM cross-account permissions during apply.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails to apply; no disruption to workloads
failure_modes.containment_strategy: Run fails fast; state is unchanged.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Update operator IAM role to include account-level S3 permissions.
failure_modes.evidence: Observed via missing permissions checks in preflight logic.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Existing workloads relying on public S3 buckets break abruptly
failure_modes.detection_method: Customer reports 403 Access Denied from end-users/applications.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Severe outage for public-facing web assets (CloudFront lacking OAC, public websites, public downloads).
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Perform complete inventory of public dependencies before applying account-level blocks.
failure_modes.evidence: Account-level block restricts all buckets regardless of individual bucket policies.

recommendation.rationale: Account-wide block public access can immediately break untracked public workloads across the entire account.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Account-wide public asset inventory scan and confirmation.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Perform comprehensive preflight checks for CloudFront distributions and public website buckets before proposing.

missing_evidence: No explicit IAM permission check before execution
No rollback automation logic in PR bundle worker

controls.name: Bucket-level Public Access Block

inventory.preflight_validation: Yes (backend/workers/services/pr_bundle_preflight.py probes website hosting and public ACLs)
inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.reversibility: Easy (CLI command). Revert bucket public access block.
safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: High for the targeted bucket if it hosts a website
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Scoped to a single bucket, but blocking public access can break web hosting or public assets. Preflight checks for website hosting help mitigate risk.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Broken static websites or public asset delivery.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Preflight scripts check website hosting but some API clients may break.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Websites breaking due to public block; IAM permission limits.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails; bucket remains public.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Verify bucket policies and IAM role for s3:PutBucketPublicAccessBlock.
failure_modes.evidence: Missing pre-execution IAM checks.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Target bucket hosts an active static website
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: If applied, active static website goes offline.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Abort apply and require manual migration to CloudFront+OAC.
failure_modes.evidence: pr_bundle_preflight.py website hosting probe.

recommendation.rationale: Applying bucket-level blocks can break existing websites or public asset delivery.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Verify no `s3:GetObject` public traffic in access logs.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Implement log analysis to prove the bucket is not legitimately serving public traffic.

missing_evidence: No automated rollback for disrupted websites

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Enabling AES256 default encryption on a bucket is generally transparent and does not break existing access patterns.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Standard AWS best practice, SSE-S3 is seamless.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: General Terraform apply failures or missing permissions.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Bucket remains unencrypted (default).
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Grant s3:PutEncryptionConfiguration to executor role.
failure_modes.evidence: IAM lacks checks.

recommendation.rationale: SSE-S3 default encryption is completely transparent to clients.

controls.name: S3 Bucket SSL Enforcement

inventory.preflight_validation: Yes (backend/workers/services/pr_bundle_preflight.py checks for existing Allow statements)
inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.reversibility: Hard (Applying new bucket policy can overwrite/delete existing statements. Rollback destroys the entire policy).
safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: High (Clients not enforcing TLS will break)
safety_assessment.concurrency_conflict_risk: High (Race conditions if multiple actors edit policy)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Applying a bucket policy for SSL enforcement can inadvertently overwrite existing access rules. Un-applying it via the provided rollback (deleting the policy entirely) is destructive.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Complete loss of legitimate access due to overwritten or deleted bucket policy.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Rollback command notes 'WARNING: removes the entire merged policy'. Preflight notes it checks for existing allows, but merge conflicts are risky.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Bucket policy conflicting limits or exceeding max policy size.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Existing bucket policy overwritten or destroyed
failure_modes.detection_method: Terraform plan shows replacement/deletion of existing policy blocks.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Legitimate applications or cross-account access abruptly lose access.
failure_modes.containment_strategy: None. Reverting destroys the entire policy.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Require manual review of existing bucket policies; do not blindly overwrite them.
failure_modes.evidence: aws_s3_bucket_policy manages the entire policy object. Rollback command explicitly notes 'removes entire merged policy'.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Consolidate policy statements.
failure_modes.evidence: Bucket policies have strict size bounds.

controls.field_execution_risks: Highly sensitive to interrupted applies, as an incomplete bucket policy overwrite could lock everyone out of the bucket permanently.

recommendation.rationale: Overwriting bucket policies with Terraform is highly destructive to existing complex merged policies.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Robust policy parser that strictly appends `Deny` statements without touching existing rules.
recommendation.biggest_risk: Irreversible deletion of critical bucket access policies.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Move away from declarative full-policy replacement (`aws_s3_bucket_policy`). Use granular API appending or CLI.

missing_evidence: No automated rollback to perfectly restore old bucket policy

controls.name: S3 Bucket Access Logging

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.rollback_mechanism_fallback_path: Manual CLI (aws s3api put-bucket-logging empty status)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Enabling access logging simply writes logs to a target bucket. Impact is minimal.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Storage cost increases in the log bucket.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Target logging bucket missing proper ACLs or policy for delivery.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Dependency missing (logging bucket lacks ACL/Policy for log delivery)
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Ensure target log bucket has LogDeliveryWrite ACL or appropriate IAM policy.
failure_modes.evidence: S3 access logging requires destination bucket permissions.

recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Verify log destination bucket exists and has correct ACLs.
recommendation.biggest_risk: Target bucket missing proper permissions, causing apply failure.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Add dependency check for log bucket ACLs.

missing_evidence: Log bucket existence verification is implicit

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: Low (Only aborts failed incomplete uploads after 7 days)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Adding a lifecycle rule to abort incomplete multipart uploads is very low risk. It cleans up unused data.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: None significant, unless legitimate uploads take > 7 days.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Lack of correct IAM permissions for s3:PutLifecycleConfiguration.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Existing data transitions or expirations immediately stop functioning.
failure_modes.containment_strategy: Terraform must be configured to append rules, not overwrite.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Use aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration carefully with existing rules.
failure_modes.evidence: aws_s3_bucket_lifecycle_configuration is authoritative for the entire bucket.

recommendation.rationale: Cleaning up incomplete multipart uploads is safe and saves cost.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Ensure terraform appends lifecycle rules rather than replacing all rules.

controls.name: S3 Bucket KMS Encryption

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.rollback_mechanism_fallback_path: Manual CLI (aws s3api put-bucket-encryption with AES256)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: Moderate (KMS requires clients to have kms:GenerateDataKey permissions)
safety_assessment.tenant_account_isolation_risk: Moderate (Cross-account access can break if KMS policies restrict it)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Switching default encryption to KMS requires all interacting IAM roles to have KMS permissions. This frequently breaks cross-account access and roles with tightly scoped inline policies.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: AccessDenied errors for principals lacking KMS cryptographic permissions.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: KMS integration is known to break transparent access if policies are not updated.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: KMS key policy denies usage to S3 or cross-account callers.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Dependency missing / KMS key policy restricts usage
failure_modes.detection_method: Terraform apply fails with KMSAccessDenied, or subsequent GetObject data calls fail.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Data is locked out to consumers that lack kms:Decrypt.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Ensure callers have KMS permissions before enforcing SSE-KMS.
failure_modes.evidence: KMS requires explicit IAM and Key Policy allows.

recommendation.rationale: Enforcing KMS encryption requires modifying cross-account role policies and KMS key policies.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Verify KMS key policies allow access to all roles currently reading the bucket.
recommendation.biggest_risk: AccessDenied for consumers lacking KMS decrypt permissions.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Perform IAM Access Analyzer checks to validate KMS access paths.

missing_evidence: No check if specified KMS key exists or is accessible

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.

inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: Low (Only affects new volumes. Might impact ancient instance types)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Region-wide default EBS encryption only applies to newly created volumes. It is non-disruptive to running instances.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Potential issues launching very old instance types that do not support encrypted EBS.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: AWS documentation confirms it only affects new volumes.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails; applies to region.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Update role with EC2 management permissions.
failure_modes.evidence: Standard IAM limits.

recommendation.rationale: Region-wide default EBS encryption only applies to newly created volumes.
recommendation.biggest_risk: Extremely old legacy instance types failing to launch.

controls.name: Security Group Hardening

inventory.preflight_validation: Yes (backend/workers/services/pr_bundle_preflight.py checks for active ENIs attached)
inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.rollback_mechanism_fallback_path: Manual terraform apply with previous.tfvars or CLI
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.reversibility: Hard (Revoking a rule in Terraform may not easily recreate the exact original rule if undone manually, tf state drift).
safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: High (Revoking 0.0.0.0/0 on port 22/3389 immediately breaks active remote administration).
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Removing public SSH/RDP access immediately closes network paths. If legitimate administrators or automated deployment scripts rely on this, they will be instantly locked out.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Immediate lockout of legitimate administrative traffic.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Preflight probes for active ENIs, but cannot determine if the 0.0.0.0/0 route is actively relied upon by humans.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Security group modified natively during PR apply; state drift lockout.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Lockout of active administrators via RDP/SSH
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Legitimate admin scripts, pipelines, and humans lose access.
failure_modes.containment_strategy: Preflight probe checks for active ENIs attached to the SG.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Require fallback allow-lists or SSM Session Manager implementation before blocking.
failure_modes.evidence: pr_bundle_preflight.py captures active ENIs but cannot verify human usage.

failure_modes.detection_method: Manual terraform rollback fails to reinstate complex legacy rules.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Extended outage while manual recreation is performed.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Save the original API output of describe-security-group-rules.
failure_modes.evidence: Terraform revokes specific rules but manual rollback is required.

controls.field_execution_risks: State loss during apply means Terraform forgets it revoked the rules. Manual intervention is required to understand what was changed.

recommendation.rationale: Revoking 0.0.0.0/0 on 22/3389 immediately terminates active SSH/RDP access.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: SSM Session Manager must be verified as active and usable before locking down ports.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Integrate SSM agent health checks into preflight.

missing_evidence: Cannot strictly verify if 0.0.0.0 is used by human admins in preflight

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Blocking public snapshots at the region level prevents accidental data exposure and does not impact private snapshot sharing.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Intentional public snapshots will begin failing.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Operates at the region level preventing new exposures.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails; applies to region.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Update role with EC2 management permissions.
failure_modes.evidence: Standard IAM limits.

recommendation.rationale: Blocking public snapshots at the region level is safe and prevents exposure.

safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Enabling Security Hub is a read-only observability change.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: AWS Config not enabled or IAM missing for Service Linked Role.

failure_modes.detection_method: Terraform apply fails or Security Hub enters inactive state.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Security Hub fails to enable or function.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Validate AWS Config enablement beforehand or bundle remediation.
failure_modes.evidence: Security Hub relies on Config.

recommendation.rationale: Enabling Security Hub is safe but depends on AWS Config.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Verify AWS Config is enabled in the region.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Bundle Config enablement or add strict preflight check.

missing_evidence: No check if AWS Config is enabled (SecurityHub prerequisite)

safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Enabling GuardDuty is an unintrusive observability change.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Missing IAM permission for Service Linked Role creation.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Missing IAM permission for Service Linked Role creation
failure_modes.detection_method: Terraform apply fails with AccessDenied for iam:CreateServiceLinkedRole.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Ensure iam:CreateServiceLinkedRole is allowed.
failure_modes.evidence: GuardDuty requires an SLR.

controls.name: CloudTrail Enablement

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.dry_run_plan_only_mode: Yes (Terraform plan phase is executed as RemediationRunExecutionPhase.plan in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.reversibility: Moderate (Leaves behind S3 buckets or roles if not fully destroyed)
safety_assessment.concurrency_conflict_risk: Moderate (Creating trails with duplicate names or existing buckets)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Enabling CloudTrail requires coordinating S3 buckets, bucket policies, and IAM roles. Failures midway can leave orphaned resources, and rollback requires careful cleanup.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Orphaned S3 buckets and IAM roles if execution fails midway.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Dependency-heavy infrastructure; tf stack is complex.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Global S3 bucket name collision or partial resource creation failure.

failure_modes.detection_method: Terraform apply fails during aws_s3_bucket create.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Partial apply. Trail might not be fully configured.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Use highly randomized bucket names with account ID.
failure_modes.evidence: Review of Terraform structure indicates an S3 bucket is created inline.

failure_modes.failure_mode: SCP / Organization trails prevent local trail creation
failure_modes.detection_method: Terraform apply fails indicating max trails reached or insufficient org permissions.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Detect Organization trails before running local remediations.
failure_modes.evidence: Common SMB scenario where AWS ProServe/MSP setup exists.

controls.field_execution_risks: Partial apply can leave orphaned S3 buckets if the trail itself fails to create.

recommendation.rationale: Dependency heavy, but generally safe if resources are uniquely named.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Check for existing organization trails or SCPs.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Ensure highly randomized S3 bucket naming and automatic rollback on failure.

missing_evidence: No S3 bucket global uniqueness verification
No rollback automation on partial trail failure

controls.name: AWS Config Enablement

inventory.post_apply_verification: Yes (backend/workers/services/post_apply_reconcile.py enqueues targeted inventory sync to verify state)

safety_assessment.concurrency_conflict_risk: Moderate (Only one default recorder allowed per region)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Setting up AWS Config requires S3 buckets, SNS topics, and IAM roles. AWS limits one configuration recorder per region, making concurrency/conflicts highly likely if one already exists.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Conflict with an already existing Configuration Recorder.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Dependency-heavy; regions are strictly limited to one recorder.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Hard AWS limit of 1 ConfigurationRecorder per region limits automation success.

failure_modes.failure_mode: Existing Configuration Recorder collision (Max 1 per region)
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails entirely.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Pre-check region for existing recorders before executing.
failure_modes.evidence: AWS hard limit on Config.

controls.field_execution_risks: Partial apply can leave orphaned S3 buckets, SNS topics, or IAM roles without a recorder.

recommendation.rationale: Safe, but frequently collides with existing recorders.
recommendation.mandatory_preconditions: Verify region does not already have a Configuration Recorder.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Add strict preflight to check `aws configservice describe-configuration-recorders`.

missing_evidence: Does not verify if a ConfigurationRecorder already exists in the region

safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Blocking public sharing of SSM documents is highly safe as public documents are extremely rare and usually a mistake.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: API change is targeted and specific to a rare edge case.

failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Remediation fails.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Ensure policy grants SSM settings update.
failure_modes.evidence: Standard API check.

recommendation.rationale: Blocking public SSM document sharing is non-disruptive.

inventory.aws_apis_resources_affected: None via API. Requires root account login.
inventory.timeout_retry_logic: Timeout=300s via subprocess in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py. No internal retry; relies on SQS visibility timeout if worker crashes.
inventory.execution_locking: Yes (Database row-level status lock via RemediationRunExecution in backend/workers/jobs/remediation_run_execution.py)

safety_assessment.reversibility: Hard (Once root access keys are deleted, they cannot be automatically restored. Requires manual root login).
safety_assessment.service_continuity_risk: Critical (If production workloads rely on root keys, they will suffer immediate, permanent outages)
safety_assessment.narrative_rationale: Root access keys should not exist, but if they do and are deleted, anything using them will break permanently. Terraform cannot manage root keys, meaning this involves manual high-stakes intervention.
safety_assessment.top_safety_risk: Irreversible destruction of root access keys actively used by legacy systems.
safety_assessment.evidence_cited: Root access requires console login; APIs cannot manage root keys. Breakage is permanent.

reliability_assessment.main_failure_driver: Cannot be automated via standard AWS APIs or Terraform.

failure_modes.detection_method: User tries to apply; fails gracefully.
failure_modes.user_customer_impact: Customer must log in manually to delete root keys.
failure_modes.containment_strategy: Remediation bundle outputs instructions instead of tf resource.
failure_modes.fix_recommendation: Provide clear manual guidance.
failure_modes.evidence: AWS prevents programmatic access to root keys.

recommendation.rationale: Standard AWS APIs cannot manage root credentials.
recommendation.biggest_risk: Tooling pretends to work but does nothing, or gives false confidence.
recommendation.most_important_fix: Convert to a purely manual workflow checklist.

test_environment_maturity.status: No explicit evidence of a fully functional, heterogeneous staging/sandbox environment that mimics SMB customer drift.
test_environment_maturity.mocks_isolated_tests: Lacking comprehensive mocks for AWS APIs. The pr_bundle logic is only tested via basic unit tests, out-of-band.
test_environment_maturity.canary_strategy: None. Bundles are run directly by the customer on their PC.
test_environment_maturity.missing_before_rollout: Need an automated e2e test suite that deploys realistic 'bad' infrastructure and asserts that the PR bundles fix them without breaking adjacent mock workloads.

concurrency_and_edge_cases.concurrent_execution: Customers run the bundle locally. The backend locks the `RemediationRunExecution` row, but there is no AWS-level locking preventing out-of-band changes.
concurrency_and_edge_cases.terraform_state_locking: State is kept locally in the `.tfstate` file on the customer's PC during the group run. No central S3 backend state locking is utilized, risking orphaned states if the PC dies.
concurrency_and_edge_cases.conflicts: High risk for S3 bucket policies or global account settings if multiple developers within the same SMB try to run different bundles simultaneously.
concurrency_and_edge_cases.rate_limits_partial_failures: Terraform handles basic AWS rate limits, but partial failures leave the local state file out of sync with the backend.

compliance_risk_boundaries.needs_audit_trails: All changes need an audit trail, but the current system does not log the exact diff applied to the backend for auditing purposes (only simple status).
compliance_risk_boundaries.iam_4_gated: No, IAM.4 requires manual console access which is completely out of band and not observable.
compliance_risk_boundaries.soc2_evidence_sufficient: No. We lack the actual applied terraform plan diffs in the database. Customer approval is also out-of-band.

field_execution_risks_overall.implementation_status: Worker relies on the terraform binary installed on the executing PC.
field_execution_risks_overall.detection_method: Terraform init/plan fails with syntax errors.
field_execution_risks_overall.recommended_guardrail: Embed a version constraint in the generated `providers.tf` (e.g., `required_version = ">= 1.3.0"`).

field_execution_risks_overall.implementation_status: No `.terraform.lock.hcl` is distributed with the bundle.
field_execution_risks_overall.detection_method: Different provider versions downloaded, potentially causing behavioral drift.
field_execution_risks_overall.recommended_guardrail: Pin the exact AWS provider version in `providers.tf`.

field_execution_risks_overall.implementation_status: State is entirely local to the temp directory on the customer PC.
field_execution_risks_overall.detection_method: Customer PC reboots during apply; next run attempts to recreate resources instead of updating them.
field_execution_risks_overall.user_impact: Duplicate resources (e.g., trying to create a second CloudTrail bucket) or complete failure to manage existing changes.
field_execution_risks_overall.recommended_guardrail: Transition to a managed remote state (e.g., S3 + DynamoDB locking) managed by the SaaS, or generate ephemeral CloudFormation instead.

field_execution_risks_overall.implementation_status: The python worker spawns a subprocess for `terraform apply`. If the user hits Ctrl+C or the machine sleeps, it hard-kills the process.
field_execution_risks_overall.detection_method: Incomplete AWS resources; `terraform.tfstate` might be corrupted.
field_execution_risks_overall.user_impact: Stranded resources or locked state requiring manual state surgery.
field_execution_risks_overall.recommended_guardrail: Catch interrupt signals, safely drain terraform, and output an exact recovery command.

field_execution_risks_overall.risk_type: Wrong credentials / wrong AWS account or region
field_execution_risks_overall.implementation_status: Relies heavily on ambient environment variables (AWS_PROFILE, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID).
field_execution_risks_overall.detection_method: Terraform apply creates resources in `us-east-1` instead of `eu-west-1`, or against Account A instead of Account B.
field_execution_risks_overall.user_impact: Applying security configurations to the wrong environment entirely.
field_execution_risks_overall.recommended_guardrail: Inject `allowed_account_ids = ["<target_id>"]` into the `aws` provider block in `providers.tf`.

guardrails: Preflight permission checks: Validate that the executing IAM role has the exact granular permissions required before modifying state.
Dependency checks: Validate AWS Config for Security Hub; log bucket ACLs for S3.9; KMS key policies for S3.15.
Backup/state snapshot strategy: Automatically output `describe` API calls to a local `rollback.json` before mutating state.
Tenant/account targeting verification: Inject `allowed_account_ids = ["<target_id>"]` into all generated Terraform `aws` providers to absolutely prevent cross-account misfires.

Terraform state locking and conflict prevention: Migrate customer PC state to a centralized SaaS-managed DynamoDB/S3 remote state bucket, or use ephemeral CloudFormation stacks instead of local Terraform.
Rollback playbooks: Generate explicit CLI rollback commands during failure.
Post-change health checks: Continuously run `pr_bundle_preflight` probes *after* apply to assert desired state.
Structured impact logging: Log specific resource IDs affected to the backend.
Kill switch: Implement a global SaaS kill-switch to pause generating all PR bundles.
Approval workflow: High-risk remediations (S3.5, EC2.53) must require explicit customer acknowledgement of risks in the UI before granting the bundle.
cross_cutting_improvements: Standard Preflight Framework: Assert IAM permissions and required dependencies natively in `pr_bundle_preflight.py` before bundle generation.
IAM Permission Probe before Apply: Perform STS simulate-principal-policy checks for exact resource ARNs.
Dependency Validation Framework: Standardize checks for prerequisites like AWS Config (for Security Hub) or KMS Key policies.
Shared Post-Apply Verification Contract: Ensure `remediation_run_execution.py` strictly polls the specific resource API after terraform apply to assert success before returning 0.
Execution Lock / Concurrency Controls: Enforce SaaS-side locking in DynamoDB to prevent two users from generating conflicting bundles simultaneously.
Rollback Playbooks / Reversibility Standards: Terraform destroy logic must be tightly coupled with the bundle, or specific CLI un-do instructions must be generated alongside the PR bundle.
Structured Telemetry Schema: Log precise Terraform Plan outputs securely back to the SaaS API.
Canary Rollout Framework: Allow 'Dry Run' plans to sit for human review internally before exposing apply capability to customers.
Approval Gating for High-Risk Remediations: Require interactive UI confirmation explicitly acknowledging risks before downloading bundles for S3.5/EC2.53.

30_days: Add explicit IAM permission checks to `pr_bundle_preflight.py`.
Enable 'Auto-Run' ONLY for the 7 designated 'Safe' controls (S3.4, S3.9, S3.11, EC2.7, EC2.182, GuardDuty.1, SSM.7).
60_days: Build centralized Remote State locking to replace customer-local `.tfstate`.
Implement 'Canary' dry-run framework for CloudTrail.1 and Config.1.
Launch 'Manual Approval' UI specifically for EC2.53 and S3.2.
90_days: Rewrite S3.5 remediation logic to properly append bucket policies without destruction.
Establish full SOC2-compliant change-management audit logs for PR bundle outcomes.
Integrate automated post-apply verification into the standard bundle payload.

issues_found.issue: Overconfident success rate estimates for dependency-heavy controls (CloudTrail.1, Config.1)
issues_found.correction_recommended: Gather explicit telemetry on the prevalence of AWS Organizations / SCPs in the SMB customer base to validate the 40-75% success rate baseline.

issues_found.issue: Weak evidence citation for exact missing IAM permissions
issues_found.correction_recommended: The review assumes missing IAM checks cause failures, but should explicitly map exactly which IAM permissions are missing from the backend worker roles vs customer roles.

issues_found.issue: Rollback capabilities are noted as manual CLI, but detailed rollback playbooks are missing from the remediation bundles
issues_found.correction_recommended: Require PR bundles to output the exact AWS CLI commands needed to reverse the change if the terraform state is lost.

definitions.safe_definition: A remediation is 'safe' if it requires zero manual rollback of stateful resources upon failure, strictly guarantees it will only execute against the specified Account ID/Region, and modifies isolated components without destructively overwriting complex merged policies (like S3 Bucket Policies).

failure_rate_targets_by_risk_class.High Risk: < 15% execution failure rate (gated by manual approval)
